title: 2026 Oscars Best Original Screenplay Prediction
text: Predicted Winner: "Sinners" - Dan Johnson's analysis of the Best Original Screenplay category identifies Ryan Coogler's "Sinners" as the likely victor. The film has built significant momentum through major industry awards, having won at Critics Choice, BAFTA, and the Writers Guild Awards. Johnson characterizes "Sinners" as featuring "Delta Gothic, blues cosmology, vampire movie, period nightmare, brother tragedy" elements that showcase Coogler's complete creative vision. The script demonstrates what Johnson calls the ability to be "authored, commercial, and culturally live all at once." The other contenders include "Sentimental Value" (noted for emotional precision), "It Was Just an Accident" (praised for tonal sophistication), "Blue Moon" (celebrated for dialogue-driven storytelling), and "Marty Supreme" (recognized for its frenetic energy). Johnson concludes that "Sinners" represents the screenplay voters can endorse confidently because it achieves world-building and thematic ambition while remaining accessible to a broad Academy audience.
